title: ActorContext
description: Identity/authorization context for the human or system actor initiating a request, consumed by policy-gate, audit, and plan-contract per docs/architecture/contract-catalog.md:20.
$comment: compat_class: closed (user decision 2026-07-12, plan §1.2 item 4 'closed 승격 5건 일괄' — virtual-cooking-map.md). Authority: approved plan §2 row 'context/actor-context'; contract-catalog.md:20 (owner forge-console-api, producer console(RBAC) -> policy-gate/audit/plan-contract, idempotency key actor_id+session, sensitivity PII); Security MUST-FIX 2 (plan §1.1 R9, user-approved) mandates the human=>tenant_id if/then below. PII minimization: identity fields (display_name/email/role) are STRUCTURALLY ABSENT from this schema by design, not merely omitted by oversight — the audit ledger stores actor_id only and identity mapping is kept in a separate store (contract-catalog.md:20 '부인방지 장기 <-> PII 최소화: ledger에는 actor_id만, 신원 매핑 분리 보관'). Do not add display_name/email/role to this schema without a fresh ADR and a compat_class/PII-classification re-review.

actor_id.$comment: tenancy-model.md:43 Identifier set — 'human or system actor'. Format OPEN (issuer runtime convention, W1 opaque) per common/identifiers $defs.actor_id.

actor_type.$comment: Discriminator driving the allOf/if/then below (Security MUST-FIX 2 / plan §1.1 R9).

session_id.$comment: Composite idempotency key part actor_id+session (contract-catalog.md:20 'Idempotency key: actor_id+session'). Concrete SEMANTICS (session lifetime, issuance, renewal) are OPEN, deferred to W2A console design (plan §7 'session_id 의미(W2A)'). Security SHOULD-1: this field MUST be an opaque session identifier, NEVER a raw session token/credential — carrying bearer tokens or other secret-shaped values here is forbidden regardless of schema-level unenforceability of that semantic constraint. | maxLength 128 aligns with the opaque-id cohort convention (identifiers $defs, all 128) — no source authorizes a larger bound; frozen at merge (closed class).

tenant_id.$comment: Optional at the base object level, but conditionally required for human actors — see allOf/if/then below (Security MUST-FIX 2, plan §1.1 R9, user-approved).

allOf.$comment: Security MUST-FIX 2 (plan §1.1 R9, user-approved 2026-07-12): a human actor MUST carry an explicit tenant_id in ActorContext — system actors (e.g. internal service accounts, cross-tenant workers) may omit it. This does not relax ADR-0014's rule that the event envelope tenant_id remains the sole authority on the event path; it constrains this context object's own shape for human-originated requests.
